title: Gloxinia villosa
taxonomy: Gloxinia villosa is classified as a species within the flowering plant family Gesneriaceae. It belongs to the genus Gloxinia. This plant is native to South America and is part of the order Lamiales. The classification of Gloxinia villosa also includes its kingdom as Plantae. This classification is based on its botanical characteristics and evolutionary relationships.

intro: Gloxinia villosa, also known as the woolly gloxinia, is a species of flowering plant in the bellflower family. Traditionally, Gloxinia villosa has been used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever reduction, coughs, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Gloxinia villosa include iridoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. The most common herbal preparations made with Gloxinia villosa include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, often used to relieve respiratory symptoms and promote relaxation.
